title: shared-clock stepping + .advance
manager: A system steps on a shared clock — one `.advance` moves every member forward together — so time is coherent across the whole system and a synthetic clock drives all of it deterministically. This is what makes multi-machine time make sense: members don't each carry their own drifting clock, they share one you control, which is exactly what makes whole-system replay reproducible.
cs: shared-clock stepping + `.advance` (§22); one clock drives all members; the deterministic time base for system replay; the alternative to per-member dispatch.
